test: out-of-sample retest of PR #27's engineering-principles loophole fix (SSoT enforcement + waste identification: sunk cost, premature abstraction)
eval_source: extracted verbatim from benchmarks/2026-07-08-ep-loophole-oos-retest/harness.workflow.js (metadata.json names it ep-loophole-oos-retest-wf_394e9703-cf3.js) TASKS array; fresh scenarios not in skills/engineering-principles/evals/evals.json
supersedes_reason: that run is hermetic:false / CONFOUNDED NULL (its own metadata.json: control-arm executors had repo/skill Read + installed-plugin access and read the treatment). ADR 0023 decision 1: 'A non-hermetic run is RECORDED (hermetic: false) as a confounded null and is never a verdict.' Its delta=0 is not evidence the loophole fix has no effect â€” it is unmeasured. THIS run is the first hermetic attempt at issue #31 item 5 and is the one whose verdict answers item 5.

executor.platform: WSL Debian (this machine's verified-today hermetic recipe; supersedes the git-bash/Windows-external-cwd recipe used by 2026-07-08-skills-hermetic and 2026-07-09-ep-remeasure-hermetic)
executor.claude_config_dir: $HOME/issue30/claude-config (clean, credentials only â€” no project/skill state to leak into the control arm)
executor.cwd: empty WSL-native scratch dir outside any repo (a Windows-side external cwd can still leak a project CLAUDE.md via cwd traversal up an ancestor path; a WSL scratch dir has no repo ancestor at all)
executor.path: $HOME/.local/bin prepended so claude resolves inside the clean config context
executor.prompt_delivery: stdin (the variadic --disallowedTools is last, so it can't eat the prompt)

treatment: with-arm --append-system-prompt = CURRENT engineering-principles SKILL.md body (frontmatter stripped) + references/ssot-enforcement.md + references/waste-identification.md, filename-headered â€” same construction as 2026-07-09-ep-remeasure-hermetic/prep.py's treatment() (body + named refs), scoped to the two refs matching this eval set's loophole areas (the original confounded harness's WITH_PREFIX used the identical two refs)
run_order: arms interleaved per rep within each eval (without-rep-N immediately followed by with-rep-N in submission order), not run as separate per-arm blocks â€” controls for temporal/load drift between paired reps
grading: grade.workflow.js + prosecute_counts.workflow.js in this dir (near-verbatim reuse of benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic's, generic over any blinded items dir), model sonnet, blind (arm withheld); met_final = min(grader_met, prosecutor_met) per ADR 0025
